iterations.hypothesis: Use BTC/USDC (listing 1) microprice as the primary fair value reference for quoting BTC/USDE (listing 28). BTC/USDC has higher volume and should give a more reliable price signal. BTC/USDE spread volatility (EWMA-smoothed) sizes the half-spread. min_spread_bps=3.0 to cover the 1.2 bps maker fee on each side.
iterations.analysis: USDC FV caused structural bias: $20 USDE-USDC premium put our asks below the USDE market mid, generating 128 adversely-selected ask fills at -1.687 bps edge.
iterations.next_action: Switch to USDE own MicropriceFairValue to eliminate basis-driven adverse selection.

iterations.hypothesis: Switch FV to BTC/USDE own microprice. Removes basis bias.
iterations.analysis: Both sides adversely selected ~0.55 bps average. USDE spread is 0.125 bps; fills happen during price sweeps. Each fill costs ~1.75 bps (1.2 maker fee + 0.55 adverse selection).
iterations.next_action: Try flow imbalance skew to detect toxic flow.

iterations.hypothesis: Add flow_bps adjustment to reduce adverse selection.
iterations.analysis: Disaster: flow_bps=2.0 > min_half=1.25 bps shifted reservation past mid, turning passive orders into aggressive market orders. 1985 taker fills at 4 bps each.
iterations.next_action: Remove flow signal. Try spread gate filter.

iterations.hypothesis: Wide USDE spreads indicate dislocation opportunity; only quote when spread > 3 bps.
iterations.analysis: Backfired: wide spreads occur during book sweeps = maximum adverse selection. Bid edge worsened from -0.504 to -1.861 bps.
iterations.next_action: Switch to USDC+basis_ewma FV for stat-arb mean-reversion edge.

iterations.hypothesis: FV = USDC + EWMA(basis). Fill when USDE deviates, profit from basis reversion. gamma=1,000,000 for A-S skew.
iterations.analysis: Catastrophic: gamma*pos*vol_raw^2/fv explodes when vol_bps=10 during sweeps. Skew=40,000,000 → bid at $11.4M. Root cause: SpreadVolatility squared makes A-S skew explosively sensitive.
iterations.next_action: Replace A-S with linear skew. Keep USDC+basis FV.

iterations.hypothesis: Replace A-S gamma with linear skew (skew_per_btc_bps * pos * fv / 10000). FV = USDC + basis_ewma (alpha=0.9990). This generates profitable fills when USDE deviates from USDC+basis.
iterations.analysis: First positive PnL (+$20)! But only 29 intents and 5 fills because FV = int(usdc_fv + basis_ewma) rarely changes integer value (basis_ewma changes by 0.001/tick, usdc_fv is integer → same price most ticks → framework only records intent changes). Sticky quotes problem.
iterations.next_action: Blend USDE (70%) with USDC+basis (30%) so USDE dominates and changes every tick, while USDC+basis provides reversion edge.

iterations.hypothesis: Blend fv = 0.7*usde_fv + 0.3*(usdc_fv + basis_ewma). USDE dominates for quote dynamism; USDC+basis provides mean-reversion edge. basis_alpha=0.99 for ~6-min half-life.
iterations.analysis: OMS unit bug: after 0.03115 BTC ask fill, oms.get_effective_quantity() returned -31,150 (micro-BTC units, not BTC). skew = 2.0*(-31150)*80447/10000 = -501,183 → reservation = 581,630 → bid at $581K → filled at market → -$50K. OMS returns position in 1e-6 BTC units (micro-BTC), not BTC. Position guard -31150 <= -5 triggered: ask suppressed. Only 2 fills.
iterations.next_action: Divide OMS position by 1e6 to convert to BTC before skew and guard calculation.

iterations.hypothesis: Clamp position used for skew to [-max_position, max_position] to prevent OMS unit-scale bugs from exploding skew.
changes: Added skew_pos = max(-max_pos, min(max_pos, position)) clamping
iterations.analysis: 100% taker fills. Clamping position_raw to [-5, 5] always produces maximum skew: ±5 * 2.0 * 80447 / 10000 = ±80, far exceeding half_spread of 12. Quotes always cross the spread. After one ask fill (OMS=-31150 → skew_pos=-5 → bid=80515 >> market_ask=80449 → taker bid), after that bid fill (position+, → ask=80376 << market_bid → taker ask). Oscillates forever.
iterations.next_action: Divide OMS position by 1e6 to get BTC, set max_position=0.5 BTC, use 'unfavorable price' guard to avoid framework slot crash from size=0.

iterations.hypothesis: Fix OMS units: position_btc = oms_position / 1e6. Use max_position=0.5 BTC with unfavorable-price guard (bid_price=fv*0.5 when long, ask_price=fv*2 when short) to avoid framework slot-collision crash from size=0 orders.
changes: Guard uses unfavorable price instead of size=0
iterations.analysis: All thresholds met (sharpe=0.000388 > 0, fill_count=85 > 50, pnl=$55 > 0) but targets far from met. 97.6% taker fills — USDC blend introduces FV lag: when USDE moves faster than USDC, blended FV overshoots current USDE price, causing our bid/ask to cross the spread. The $55 PnL is BTC appreciation on accumulated long position (0.67 BTC), not trading edge. Fills only in first 7 hours (00:10-07:22), then guard suppresses after accumulating 0.67 BTC long and market doesn't come back to us. Real per-fill edge is negative.
iterations.next_action: Remove USDC blend from FV entirely: use fv = usde_fv only (always tracks market, never lags). Use basis deviation as a reservation ADJUSTMENT (signed skew) rather than FV component: when USDE is cheap vs historical basis, skew reservation up to buy more; when expensive, skew down to sell. This preserves the mean-reversion signal without introducing FV lag.
